title: Question: Verilator 3.843 : Getting compile error mapping DPI uint32_t in C prototype to "int unsigned" in SV
body:
---


Author Name: **Alex Lee**
Original Redmine Message: 966 from https://www.veripool.org



---

The SV file contains an import DPI-C function prototype like this:

import "DPI-C" function void update ( input int unsigned count ) ;

The C prototype looks like this:

void update ( uint32_t count ) ;


I am getting a compile error message using verilator 3.843:

In file included from ./Vtop.h:12, from <>.cpp:<>:
.Vtop__Dpi.h:17: error: declaration of C function 'void update(int)' conflicts with 
<>.hpp:<>: error: previous declaration 'void update(uint32_t)' here


It had previously compiled without errors in verilator 3.810.

What SV type should I declare in order to properly map to uint32_t?
